template_name: EU Code of Practice on Marking and Labelling AI-Generated Content

provisions.description: AI system providers shall embed secured machine-readable metadata in AI-generated outputs, using recognized provenance standards (e.g., C2PA manifests), to enable automated detection and verification of synthetic content origin

sub_provisions.description: C2PA manifests are the encouraged format for secured metadata, providing cryptographic binding of provenance to content
Secured metadata must be interoperable across platforms and content formats

evaluation.message: At least one transparency_marking record is required — EU Labelling Code of Practice Layer 1 mandates secured machine-readable metadata in AI-generated content
Every transparency_marking record must specify marking_scheme_id — Layer 1 requires identification of the provenance standard used (e.g., C2PA, IPTC)
Every transparency_marking record must specify the metadata_container (e.g., c2pa_manifest, exif, id3, xmp) describing where secured metadata is embedded per Layer 1 interoperability requirements
Transparency marking records should specify the content modality (text, image, audio, video, multimodal) for Layer 1 secured metadata
Transparency marking records should be linked to a subject entity to identify which AI system produces the marked content

provisions.description: AI system providers shall apply imperceptible digital watermarks to AI-generated content that are robust against compression, cropping, format conversion, and other common transformations

sub_provisions.description: Watermarks must be robust against compression, cropping, and format conversion while remaining imperceptible to human users

evaluation.message: At least one transparency_marking record is required — EU Labelling Code of Practice Layer 2 mandates digital watermarking of AI-generated content
At least one transparency_marking record must have watermark_applied set to true — Layer 2 mandates imperceptible digital watermarking of AI-generated content
Transparency marking records with watermarks should specify watermark_family (e.g., spectral_embedding, robust-imperceptible) for robustness verification
Transparency marking records should include an attachment documenting watermark robustness parameters and test results per Layer 2 robustness requirements

provisions.description: AI system providers shall make available free detection and verification APIs that return confidence scores, provenance data, and synthetic content indicators, enabling downstream platforms and deployers to verify AI-generated content

sub_provisions.description: Detection APIs must return confidence scores indicating the likelihood that content is AI-generated
Detection and verification APIs must be made available free of charge to downstream deployers and platforms

evaluation.message: At least one transparency_marking record is required — EU Labelling Code of Practice Detection Commitment mandates documented detection and verification capabilities
Every transparency_marking record must specify verification_method_ref — the Detection Commitment requires a documented reference to the detection or verification API endpoint
Transparency marking records should be linked to a subject entity to identify which AI system's detection API is documented

provisions.description: AI system providers and deployers shall apply the EU uniform AI icon ('AI'/'IA'/'KI' acronym) to AI-generated content, positioned consistently and visible at first exposure, as required by Art. 50 of the EU AI Act

sub_provisions.description: The uniform icon must be positioned consistently and be visible at the first exposure to the content

evaluation.message: At least one disclosure_labeling record is required — EU Labelling Code of Practice Icon Commitment mandates the EU uniform AI icon on AI-generated content
Every disclosure_labeling record must specify the presentation method for the EU uniform icon (e.g., visible_icon_plus_text) per Icon Commitment placement requirements
Disclosure labeling records should specify the locale for language-appropriate icon variant selection (AI/IA/KI) per EU multilingual requirements

provisions.description: AI system providers are responsible for the technical implementation of content marking, including embedding secured metadata (Layer 1) and watermarks (Layer 2) at the point of generation

evaluation.message: At least one transparency_marking record is required — providers must document their technical marking implementation at the point of content generation
Provider transparency_marking records must specify marking_scheme_id to identify the technical marking standard implemented
Provider transparency marking records should be linked to a subject entity to identify which AI system implements the marking

provisions.description: AI system deployers are responsible for user-facing labels and disclosures, ensuring end users are informed that content is AI-generated through the EU uniform icon and appropriate contextual disclosures

evaluation.message: At least one disclosure_labeling record is required — deployers must document their user-facing AI content disclosure implementation
Deployer disclosure_labeling records must specify the presentation method used for user-facing AI content disclosure
Deployer disclosure labeling records should confirm first_exposure_visible to verify the disclosure is visible at the moment of initial user contact per EU Labelling CoP requirements
Deployer disclosure labeling records should be linked to a subject entity to identify which deployed AI system the disclosures apply to
